task_completion.description: Verify the messy inventory CSV data is opened in a spreadsheet application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) is open, displaying the raw inventory data with inconsistent formatting across ~20 rows.

task_completion.description: Verify the data has been cleaned and standardized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet columns for dates, locations, and categories show consistent formatting (e.g., uniform date formats like MM/DD/YYYY, consistent capitalization).

task_completion.description: Verify expiration statuses have been calculated for the items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column exists that explicitly states the expiration status of the items (e.g., 'Expired', 'Good', or days remaining). Most items should be marked as expired.

task_completion.description: Verify conditional formatting is applied to highlight expired/expiring items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells or entire rows containing expired items are visually highlighted using background colors (e.g., red or yellow) or distinct text styling.

task_completion.description: Verify a report containing waste pattern analysis and actionable insights is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section of the spreadsheet, a text box, or a separate sheet contains written text analyzing the waste patterns (e.g., noting the high volume of expired items) and providing actionable recommendations.

task_completion.description: Verify the final report is saved to the correct location with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet application's title bar shows 'inventory_audit_results.xlsx', or a file manager/terminal window shows this file successfully saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with a GUI spreadsheet application to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a spreadsheet GUI (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) being actively used, rather than just a terminal window running a Python script to generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the conditional formatting is applied using actual spreadsheet styling features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The highlighting should be visible as actual cell background colors or font colors in the spreadsheet, not just a text column saying 'Color: Red'.

integrity.description: Verify the waste pattern analysis contains actual observations rather than copied prompt text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The analysis text should reflect the actual data (e.g., mentioning specific categories or the high number of expired items) rather than just repeating the instructions 'analyze waste patterns and provide actionable insights'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 20 items with 6 columns. The task execution date is 2026-03-07. Because the CSV contains hardcoded historical dates, 19 of the 20 items are actually expired as of this date, and 1 item has no expiration. The VLM should expect to see 19 items marked and highlighted as expired. The final output must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/inventory_audit_results.xlsx.
